name: gmail-postmaster-tools
description: Query Gmail Postmaster Tools (v2 API) — domains, traffic metrics, and sender compliance — using your own Google OAuth client.
long_description: Exposes the Gmail Postmaster Tools API v2 as MCP tools. This is a bring-your-own-credentials extension: you create a Google Cloud OAuth client of type "Desktop app", enable the Gmail Postmaster Tools API, and paste the Client ID and Client Secret into this extension's configuration. Sign-in uses an OAuth 2.0 authorization-code + PKCE flow over a 127.0.0.1 loopback redirect; tokens are cached locally and refreshed automatically. Then list your registered domains, read sender compliance verdicts (SPF/DKIM/DMARC, alignment, encryption, one-click unsubscribe, user-reported spam rate), and query traffic metrics (spam rate, auth success, TLS encryption, delivery errors, feedback loop) over any date range.

google_client_id.title: Google OAuth Client ID
google_client_id.description: From a Google Cloud 'Desktop app' OAuth client (APIs & Services > Credentials). See the README for setup steps.

google_client_secret.title: Google OAuth Client Secret
google_client_secret.description: The client secret for the same Desktop app OAuth client. Stored locally; required by Google even for desktop apps.

tools.name: gpt_authenticate
tools.description: Sign in to Gmail Postmaster Tools via your Google OAuth client (opens the browser).

tools.name: gpt_auth_status
tools.description: Show current sign-in status and token expiry.

tools.name: gpt_sign_out
tools.description: Delete the cached Google tokens (sign out).

tools.name: list_domains
tools.description: List domains registered in your Postmaster Tools account.

tools.name: get_domain
tools.description: Get metadata for one registered domain.

tools.name: get_compliance_status
tools.description: Get sender compliance verdicts for a domain.

tools.name: query_domain_stats
tools.description: Query Gmail traffic metrics for a domain over a date range.
